synthesis.summary: P4: LLM MCP scheduled-job cron pods failed to spawn on Betconstruct cluster nodes over the weekend. No partner impact — internal AI-team tooling.

synthesis.summary: Recurring H2Bet nightly ClickHouse-writer pileup, previously attributed to a payload type-mismatch bug. This cycle Matteo surfaced a new, more fundamental root cause: the ClickHouse EBS volume's gp3 throughput ceiling (125MB/s) is hit during scheduled analytics scans, stalling writes fleet-wide (~40 pods crash-looping ~10x/hour for 14+ days). He has a concrete one-command fix ready but lacks the IAM permission to run it, and flagged the same risk exists on 35+ other ClickHouse volumes fleet-wide. Patrik and five senior engineers joined the channel the same moment this was posted.
key_events: 17 Jul 14:34 UTC status moved to Monitoring; Andrea's 'next week' check-in point set

20 Jul 13:36 CEST Matteo posts a new, deeper root-cause finding: the h2bet ClickHouse 16TB gp3 volume is capped at default 125MB/s throughput; scheduled Hex analytics scans pin disk reads at that ceiling for 5+ min, writes collapse, and the clickhouse-writer fleet (~40 pods) times out and crash-loops together — happening ~10x/hour for 14+ days
20 Jul 13:36 CEST Matteo proposes a one-command live fix (AWS EC2 modify-volume, ~$15/mo) but flags he can't run it himself — his SOC role lacks ec2:ModifyVolume

20 Jul 13:36 CEST Matteo flags this is fleet-wide: 35+ ClickHouse volumes across eu1/eu2/betconstruct/wt-ltk/everymatrix clusters share the same gp3 default-throughput risk (incl. roobet 12.9TB, gamdom 4.9TB, padisahbet 4.4TB) — h2bet is just the first to become symptomatic
20 Jul 13:36 CEST Patrik Potocki, Stephen Riolo, Max Holdaway, Agustín Castaño, Curt Healey and Andrew Pirotta all join the channel
20 Jul run (16:39 CEST cycle): zero new Slack activity since Matteo's 13:36 CEST fleet-wide finding; the ec2:ModifyVolume permission grant / decision still not confirmed, ~1h since flagged
proposals: Schema/type-mismatch fix (ActionEvent.Status int vs string) — still not implemented, ownership handed to Matteo as of 17 Jul
open_breaches: A4/D12: a 14+ day recurring crash-loop is now root-caused with a ready one-line fix, but execution is blocked purely on an IAM permission gap — needs a same-day decision, not silent drift, especially given the fleet-wide exposure Matteo flagged (35+ volumes)
synthesis.why: 🟡 — this is very fresh (posted ~1h before this digest run) so a decision hasn't had time to land yet, but it's an important emerging pattern: a long-standing (14+ day) recurring problem now has a concrete, cheap, ready fix that's purely blocked on access, plus a fleet-wide risk across 35+ volumes that no one has acted on yet. Patrik and several senior engineers are already in the channel — worth confirming next run that the h2bet fix was applied and that someone owns reviewing the other volumes.
synthesis.suggested_action: Grant Matteo (or have someone with access run) the ec2:ModifyVolume fix for h2bet today; assign an owner to review throughput settings across the other 34 similarly-provisioned ClickHouse volumes flagged fleet-wide.

B5: still holds — zero evidence of any BetConstruct response or outreach progress since 18 Jun
C10/D15: still holds — Intercom conversation remains closed while incident.io stays open P1 with no incident.io-side explanation of the closure or REW-176 status
synthesis.why: All three breaches behind the 🚨 flag (A2/A4, B5, C10/D15) still hold with zero change this cycle. This reflects the 16 Jul management note (H2Bet accepted the workaround) but the note does not resolve the underlying breaches. Now ~55 days open.
synthesis.suggested_action: Force a decision point: get Rewards-team capacity committed to REW-176 with a hard date, or explicitly accept the ~300/week duplicate-grant cost until mid-August and say so in the record.

management_notes.note: Andrea: correction — H2Bet ACCEPTED the interim workaround (discussed in the meeting with them and our Partner Manager, early Jul); that acceptance is why REW-176 was opened on 9 Jul. The 28 May rejection was the earlier 503-as-success proposal only. No sign-off is pending from H2Bet.

INC-14567.name: Sub agent potential issue: dashboard-admin agent type not allowed

synthesis.summary: llm-agent service rejected a dashboard-admin sub-agent invocation (allowlist restriction), affecting only a PR-preview build (PR #467) on brand rhino — no production/customer impact. Jeremy Curmi confirmed the full root cause this cycle (invalid subagent_type not caught as a runtime error, unlike schema errors) and proposed a durable fix; incident downgraded P3→P4 and moved Investigating→Fixing the same session.
key_events: 17 Jul 22:13 CEST alert fires, incident channel created
17 Jul 22:24-22:26 CEST Andrea Azzopardi assigns Jeremy Curmi as Incident Lead, notes merging related alarms and the need for stronger incident ownership
20 Jul 09:41 CEST Jeremy Curmi formally accepts the incident, status Triage → Investigating
20 Jul run: zero new Slack activity; still Investigating since 09:41 CEST accept, ~3h stale — within normal working grace period
20 Jul run (14:39 cycle): zero new Slack activity; still Investigating since 09:41 CEST accept, now ~5h stale
20 Jul 14:47 CEST Jeremy Curmi posts full root-cause writeup: PR #467 preview build only, no customer impact, proposes durable fix (non-fatal invalid-subagent_type handling) plus two mitigations
20 Jul 14:55 CEST severity downgraded P3→P4, status Investigating→Fixing; Jeremy notes 'not something happening on all clients, still testing' / 'not really an incident'
synthesis.why: No breach — root cause fully confirmed with a clear fix plan, scope narrowed to a non-production preview build, severity honestly downgraded to P4 same session. Prior B5 staleness flag is resolved by this cycle's activity.

synthesis.summary: LeonBets: one user (Petr) couldn't create a new segment (missing filter dropdown, 403 in browser console) after a new Unleash flag (crm-api-enforce-endpoint-permissions) started enforcing endpoint-level permissions on GET /ActivityManager/singularity/features. Matteo Rapisarda root-caused it to a missing Singularity:Features:View permission on Petr's account, granted it directly, and asked him to confirm — all within ~1 hour of the alert.
key_events: 20 Jul 13:33 UTC (15:33 CEST) alert fires, escalation to L2 expired, Matteo self-acknowledges and takes lead
20 Jul 16:18-16:27 CEST Matteo diagnoses: GET .../singularity/features requires Singularity:Features:View, which Petr's role lacks; the new Unleash flag now enforces this at the endpoint
20 Jul 16:33-16:34 CEST Matteo grants the permission directly, updates incident.io, moves status to Waiting on Partner
20 Jul 16:39 CEST channel renamed to reflect final incident name — no substantive change

promises: asked Petr to confirm UI works again — pending confirmation

proposals: Grant Singularity:Features:View to Petr's account — applied 16:33 CEST
synthesis.why: No breach — fast, correct root-cause diagnosis and fix within ~1 hour, no need to touch the underlying feature flag; awaiting routine partner confirmation.

synthesis.summary: Recurring Meridianbet Kafka/RabbitMQ lag alert cluster (self-named 'the usual' by the reporter, reflecting a known recurring pattern). Matteo merged 8 related alerts into this one incident; all 8 attached alerts resolved on their own within ~40 minutes of the incident opening.
key_events: 20 Jul 13:03 UTC (15:03 CEST) incident opens, Matteo self-assigns lead, merges 6 related alert-incidents (14846, 14848-14853) into this one
20 Jul 15:37-15:43 CEST INC-14856 also merged in; all 8 attached alerts resolve
synthesis.why: No breach — self-resolved within ~40 minutes, P4, no partner impact. Named 'the usual' by the team, confirming this is a known recurring low-severity pattern rather than a new problem — worth noting as an A4 candidate for a permanent fix if it keeps recurring, but not a breach on its own.

synthesis.summary: Cloudflare WAF rule (attack-ML scoring) was blocking the calculated-segment-field save endpoint (AggCalculatedFields/Upsert) on Kyngs Production with 403s — the same recurring false-positive class as three prior documented incidents (trigger expressions, action fields, unsubscribe HTML). Matteo Rapisarda root-caused it fast and, because it touched a security WAF rule, deliberately looped in Patrik Potocki and Stephen Riolo for review despite Patrik being on vacation; Patrik responded within minutes, Carl Bondin gave a second approval, PR #19 merged and deployed via Spacelift — reporters asked to retry. ~1h42m from alert to fix deployed.
key_events: 20 Jul 12:55 UTC (14:55 CEST) alert fires, Matteo acknowledges and takes lead

20 Jul 15:18-15:33 CEST Matteo identifies the WAF rule (ref ea2a7c8f) and root-causes the missing Upsert exclusion, flags honeypot auto-ban escalation risk, deliberately asks for Patrik/Stephen review given the security-sensitive change
20 Jul 15:33-15:43 CEST Patrik Potocki (on vacation) and Stephen Riolo respond within minutes; Patrik authorises the change
20 Jul 15:55-16:22 CEST PR #19 opened, reviewed (Carl Bondin approval), merged
20 Jul 16:35-16:38 CEST Spacelift plan deployed, status moved to Waiting on Partner, reporters asked to retry

promises: asked reporters (Kyngs CRM users) to retry saving — pending confirmation

proposals: PR #19: exclude AggCalculatedFields/Upsert from WAF attack-block rule — merged and deployed 16:22-16:37 CEST
synthesis.why: No breach — P2 handled fast and thoroughly (~1h42m to deployed fix), with proper security-change review discipline maintained even under time pressure and even though the approver was on vacation. Textbook handling.
